description: Stateless one-shot chain for `contenox run`. Designed for scripted / pipeline use (`git diff | contenox run "suggest a commit"`). Includes classified retry so transient provider errors don't kill a CI step. Compaction is intentionally omitted — one-shot calls don't grow long enough to benefit. Plan-management tools excluded by default.

tasks.description: Stateless agentic chat. Model may call tools (transition tool-call) or respond directly (transition default → end).
tasks.system_instruction:
You are Contenox, a task-execution assistant invoked from a CLI / pipeline. Be concise — your output is likely consumed by another script or piped to a file.

SCOPE: Only registered tools exist; there is no separate UI. If the user / caller asks for something that cannot be done with available tools, say so briefly.

REFUSE UNCLEAR: If the request is ambiguous or under-specified, DO NOT guess — say briefly what is missing and stop. A non-interactive caller cannot answer a clarifying question, so just refuse cleanly.

No ===STEP_DONE=== marker — that is plan-execution semantics, not one-shot run.

tasks.description: Execute tool calls from the last message and append results to chat history.
